thoughts.title: Why every small business needs custom nameservers
thoughts.excerpt: Using your host's default nameservers is like handing out a generic business card. Custom nameservers turn hosting into a brand signal.
thoughts.body:
When a client points their domain to `ns1.famtastichosting.com`, every DNS lookup becomes a tiny ad.

Custom nameservers also make future migrations invisible: the client never has to touch their registrar again.

Three reasons to care:
1. **Brand consistency** — your name is on every lookup.
2. **White-label support** — the client sees you, not GoDaddy.
3. **Future flexibility** — move infrastructure without disruption.

This is one of the quietest upgrades that makes a hosting business feel premium.

thoughts.title: The fastest way to prove value to a local service client
thoughts.excerpt: Before selling a website, show a before/after of their current site loading on a phone.
thoughts.body:
Local service businesses don't buy websites. They buy more calls.

The fastest proof:
- Open their current site on a 3-year-old phone.
- Record a Lighthouse mobile report.
- Show a 60-second mockup of a faster, cleaner page.

That combo turns a pitch into a conversation about results.

collections.title: Hosting Lessons
collections.description: Practical notes from running a white-label hosting business.
